title: State of the Planet
description: News from the Columbia Climate School

items.title: Indonesia May Soon Lose Its Last Glaciers
items.description: Scientists estimate that Indonesia will lose its two remaining glaciers by 2030—a warning for glaciers around the world.

items.title: Climate Finance in the Multipolar Era
items.description: Climate finance in the multipolar era will be driven less by collective targets and more by the need to manage geopolitical security risks in a less stable world.

items.title: A New Study Explains How Carbon Dioxide Cools the Upper Atmosphere—and Warms Earth Below
items.description: Researchers have solved a long-standing atmospheric puzzle: how rising carbon dioxide cools the stratosphere even as it warms Earth’s surface and lower atmosphere.

items.title: Why We’re All on the Same Team in the Fight Against Climate Change
items.description: Campbell Award winner Marina Saguar Urquiola is helping to solve climate change through adaptation finance.

items.title: “Near-miss” Tsunami in Alaskan Cruise Area Offers Lessons for Steep Landscapes Near Glaciers
items.description: As glaciers retreat in warming regions, the risk of related hazards can increase, and improved monitoring may help reduce some of those dangers, according to a new study.

items.title: You Asked: Lamont and Climate School Experts Tackle Reader Questions on Science and Sustainability
items.description: We invite readers to share their most pressing questions about climate, science and sustainability.

items.title: Lamont Paleoclimatologist Maureen Raymo Receives the 2026 Nemmers Prize
items.description: Raymo received the award for her pioneering development of hypotheses that explain climate change across Earth’s history, and her educational leadership in the Earth system sciences.

items.title: Melanie Nakagawa To Speak at Columbia Climate School Graduation
items.description: Columbia Climate School is thrilled to announce that Melanie Nakagawa, Chief Sustainability Officer at Microsoft, will deliver the keynote address at the Climate School’s Class Day ceremony.

items.title: Geologist Mike Kaplan Named 2026 Guggenheim Fellow
items.description: Kaplan studies the ways ice sheets, mountain glaciers, climates and landscapes changed in the past.

items.title: Climate Work Is Personal for This Class Day Speaker
items.description: Annika Bellot focuses on international law and decarbonization efforts to help save small island states like Dominica, where she grew up.
